config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society’s interests should automatically come before individual rights, because that easily becomes an excuse to silence people and abuse power. In my view, a just society protects basic individual rights first, then balances them with the common good when there is a real and necessary reason. From my perspective, collective goals matter, but they should never erase personal dignity, freedom, and due process.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang harus menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar, bukan hal yang bisa dikorbankan kapan saja. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibuktikan secara ketat dan dibatasi hanya pada situasi yang benar-benar perlu, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk menekan orang. Kalau hak individu bisa dikalahkan begitu saja, masyarakat justru kehilangan fondasi moral dan politiknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically override individual rights, because once that rule is accepted, it becomes very easy for authorities to justify almost anything. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to harmony, order, or the common good can be used to pressure people into silence, so I do not trust society-first arguments unless they are tightly limited and accountable. A community does matter, especially in crises like public health or disaster response, but even then the burden should be on proving why a rights restriction is necessary and temporary. For me, the stronger principle is that society is healthiest when it protects individual dignity first and only limits rights in rare, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu harus tetap menjadi titik awal, bukan dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat secara otomatis. Alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari kekuasaan yang sewenang-wenang, jadi saya sangat tidak percaya pada argumen yang memberi prioritas umum tanpa batas yang ketat. Kepentingan publik memang bisa penting dalam keadaan darurat, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus tinggi, sementara pembatasannya harus sementara, perlu, dan bisa diawasi. Kalau masyarakat diberi kuasa untuk menggeser hak individu terlalu mudah, yang terjadi bukan ketertiban yang sehat, melainkan penyalahgunaan yang dibungkus bahasa moral.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for harmony” or “for the common good” can become a convenient excuse to pressure dissenters and weaken accountability, so I do not trust broad society-first claims. Public interests do matter in emergencies, but the burden must always be on proving necessity, proportionality, and clear limits. A healthy society protects individual rights first, because once those rights are treated as secondary, abuse becomes much easier to justify.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; bagi saya di Amerika, hak individu harus menjadi dasar, bukan dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat secara otomatis. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana ketika pembatasan sementara bisa dibenarkan, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus sangat tinggi dan pengawasannya ketat. Argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, membatasi kebebasan berbicara, dan memberi ruang bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling kuat melindungi hak dasar sambil membatasi negara secara jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
